items.q: What is the focus of virtue ethics?
items.correct: On character and habits — becoming the kind of person who acts well, aiming at eudaimonia (flourishing) through practising virtues that lie between extremes.
items.misconception: Virtue ethics tells you which specific actions are right and wrong.
items.random_wrong: Virtue ethics is about following the Ten Commandments.
items.why: Virtue ethics asks 'what kind of person should I be?' rather than 'what should I do?'. Students try to extract action-rules from it, missing its character-based agent-focused nature.

items.q: What is the difference between Sunni and Shia Islam?
items.correct: They split over who should lead the Muslim community after Muhammad: Sunnis accepted Abu Bakr and elected caliphs; Shias believed leadership should follow Muhammad's bloodline through Ali.
items.misconception: Sunnis and Shias believe in different gods.
items.random_wrong: Sunnis follow the Quran and Shias follow the Bible.
items.why: Both share the Quran, prophet and core beliefs. The original split was political — about succession — and theological differences developed later. Students inflate it into a different religion.

items.correct: The Islamic declaration of faith: 'There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is the messenger of Allah' — affirming monotheism and Muhammad's prophethood.
items.misconception: The Shahada is the requirement to pray five times a day.
items.random_wrong: The Shahada is the Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca.
items.why: Students confuse the five pillars. Shahada is the declaration, Salah is prayer, Hajj is pilgrimage — each is a distinct pillar with a different function.

items.q: Which church teaches Purgatory as a state of cleansing before heaven?
items.why: Correct answer: Roman Catholic. “Baptist” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Within how many hours, ideally, are Muslims buried after death?
items.why: Correct answer: 24. “12” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Within how many hours of death should Muslims ideally be buried?
items.why: The real figure is 24. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
